items.title: Former Andhra Pradesh CM Nadendla Bhaskara Rao Dies at 90
items.whyInNews: Former Chief Minister of undivided Andhra Pradesh Nadendla Bhaskara Rao passed away on April 22, 2026, in Hyderabad at the age of 90 while undergoing treatment for age-related ailments.
items.summary: Nadendla Bhaskara Rao, co-founder of the Telugu Desam Party (TDP), served as Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh for 31 days in 1984 during a political crisis against N.T. Rama Rao. He assumed office with support from a faction of TDP legislators and Congress (I) while NTR was in the United States for surgery. His government collapsed after a mass “Save Democracy” movement, leading to NTR’s reinstatement.
keyPoints: Nadendla Bhaskara Rao died on April 22, 2026, in Hyderabad at age 90
Served as Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister for 31 days in 1984
Co-founded Telugu Desam Party (TDP) in 1982 with N.T. Rama Rao
Was Finance Minister in the first TDP government after 1983 Assembly elections
importantPoints: Telugu Desam Party (TDP) founded in 1982
Governor’s role in government formation under Article 164 of the Constitution
1984 Andhra Pradesh political crisis and ‘Save Democracy’ movement
Shortest-serving Chief Ministers in Indian political history
Highlights issues of political stability and constitutional morality in India
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC Polity under topics like State Government, Chief Ministers, Governor’s role, and political developments in Indian states

items.title: India Aims to Establish Blood Centre in Every District by December 2026
items.whyInNews: On April 22, 2026, the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced a national target to establish at least one blood centre in every district by December 2026 after reviewing blood transfusion services across states and Union Territories.
items.summary: India plans to ensure universal access to safe blood by setting up at least one blood centre in every district by December 2026. A national review revealed that nearly 10% of districts still lack blood centres and highlighted gaps in licensing, infrastructure, and digital integration. The government aims to improve compliance, expand voluntary blood donation, and eliminate transfusion-transmitted infections.
keyPoints: India targets at least one blood centre in every district by December 2026
Nearly 10% of districts currently lack a blood centre
Review conducted by Ministry of Health and Family Welfare via video conference with states and UTs
Performance assessed using 10 KPIs based on eRaktKosh, BBMS and CDSCO data
importantPoints: National AIDS Control Organisation (NACO) plays a key role in blood safety and policy implementation
Focus on eliminating transfusion-transmitted infections (TTIs)
Digital platforms include eRaktKosh and Blood Bank Management System (BBMS)
Push for 100% licensing compliance and voluntary blood donation
Improves healthcare accessibility and strengthens public health infrastructure in India
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Studies under Health, Government Schemes, and Social Sector initiatives, especially public health infrastructure and digital health systems

items.title: Six Women Win 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize Across Six Regions
items.whyInNews: On April 21, 2026, six women activists from Colombia, Nigeria, Papua New Guinea, South Korea, the United Kingdom, and the United States were awarded the Goldman Environmental Prize 2026, marking the first all-women cohort since the award’s inception in 1989.
items.summary: The 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize was awarded to six grassroots women environmental activists for their contributions to climate action and biodiversity conservation. The winners include Iroro Tanshi (Nigeria), Borim Kim (South Korea), Sarah Finch (UK), Theonila Roka Matbob (Papua New Guinea), Alannah Acaq Hurley (USA), and Yuvelis Morales Blanco (Colombia). Each awardee represents one of the world’s six regions and receives $200,000.
keyPoints: Six women activists won the Goldman Environmental Prize 2026
First all-women cohort since the prize was established in 1989
Each winner represents one of six global regions and receives $200,000
Award recognises grassroots efforts in climate change and biodiversity conservation
importantPoints: Goldman Environmental Prize established in 1989 by Richard and Rhoda Goldman
Often referred to as the 'Green Nobel Prize'
Awarded annually to grassroots environmental activists worldwide
Focus areas include climate change, biodiversity conservation, and environmental justice
Highlights the growing role of women in global environmental leadership
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under Awards and Honours, especially international environmental awards and notable global recognitions

items.title: NASA Completes Nancy Grace Roman Telescope Assembly Ahead of 2026 Launch
items.whyInNews: On April 22, 2026, NASA confirmed that the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has completed final assembly at the Goddard Space Flight Center, Maryland, and is preparing for launch in 2026.
items.summary: The Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, NASA’s next-generation observatory after Hubble and the James Webb Space Telescope, has been fully assembled. Designed to scan vast regions of space in visible and near-infrared light, it can map the sky 1,000 times faster than Hubble. The mission aims to study dark energy, galaxy formation, and detect exoplanets using advanced instruments like a coronagraph.
keyPoints: NASA completed assembly of Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope in April 2026 at Goddard Space Flight Center
Telescope will map space 1,000 times faster than Hubble with images covering 100 times larger area
Primary mirror size is 2.4 metres, similar to Hubble Space Telescope
Will be launched aboard SpaceX Falcon Heavy to Lagrange Point 2 (around 1 million miles from Earth)
importantPoints: Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope is NASA’s next major space observatory after Hubble and James Webb
Focus on studying dark energy and dark matter through large-scale cosmic mapping
Equipped with coronagraph for direct imaging of exoplanets
Lagrange Point 2 (L2) provides stable observation environment for space telescopes
Mission will help understand universe expansion and structure formation
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Science under Space Technology, NASA missions, telescopes, and concepts like dark energy, exoplanets, and Lagrange points

items.title: Cabinet Extends PMGSY-III Till March 2028 with Increased Budget
items.whyInNews: The Union Cabinet recently approved the continuation and budget enhancement of Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana-III (PMGSY-III) beyond March 2025, extending it till March 2028 to strengthen rural connectivity.
items.summary: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY), launched in 2000, aims to provide all-weather road connectivity to unconnected rural habitations. Under PMGSY-III, the focus is on consolidating rural road networks and linking villages with markets, schools, and healthcare centres. The scheme has already constructed about 800,000 km of roads and connected around 180,000 habitations.
keyPoints: PMGSY-III extended till March 2028 with enhanced budget approval by Union Cabinet
Scheme launched in 2000 to provide all-weather rural road connectivity
Around 800,000 km roads built and 180,000 habitations connected so far
Funding pattern changed from 100% central to 60:40 Centre-State share since 2015-16
importantPoints: PMGSY is a flagship rural infrastructure scheme of Government of India
PMGSY-III focuses on connecting rural areas with markets, schools, and healthcare facilities
Completion timeline: March 2028 for most works and March 2029 for bridges in hilly areas
Supports rural economic growth, employment generation, and reduced logistics cost
Contributes to the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047 and rural-urban integration
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC GS under Infrastructure, Government Schemes, and Rural Development; frequently asked in exams regarding PMGSY features, launch year, and objectives

items.title: India–Republic of Korea Unveil Joint Strategic Vision 2026–2030
items.whyInNews: India and the Republic of Korea (ROK) unveiled the Joint Strategic Vision 2026–2030 during the state visit of South Korean President Lee Jae Myung to India, aiming to deepen their Special Strategic Partnership.
items.summary: The India–ROK Joint Strategic Vision (2026–2030) expands bilateral ties beyond economic cooperation to include Indo-Pacific security, emerging technologies, defence, and climate action. It aligns India’s Act East Policy with South Korea’s New Southern Policy and introduces initiatives in digital payments, space cooperation, and supply chain resilience. The vision also focuses on addressing trade imbalance and enhancing multilateral coordination.
keyPoints: Joint Strategic Vision 2026–2030 announced during South Korean President Lee Jae Myung’s visit to India
Alignment of India’s Act East Policy with South Korea’s New Southern Policy
Launch of India–ROK Economic Security Dialogue to diversify supply chains
Integration of NPCI (India) with Korea’s KFTC for real-time cross-border payments
importantPoints: India–ROK Special Strategic Partnership upgraded to comprehensive strategic cooperation
Defence cooperation through joint R&D and Defence Industry Joint Committee
ISRO–KASA Joint Working Group for space collaboration
Cooperation under Article 6.2 of Paris Agreement for carbon trading
South Korea joins International Solar Alliance (ISA); India joins Global Green Growth Institute (GGGI)
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC GS under International Relations, Indo-Pacific strategy, bilateral agreements, and global partnerships involving India

items.title: Japan Lifts Arms Export Ban, Allows Lethal Weapons Sales in 2026
items.whyInNews: In April 2026, Japan scrapped its ‘five-category’ restriction on arms exports, allowing the export of lethal weapons such as missiles, destroyers, and fighter jets to select partner countries.
items.summary: Japan has made a historic shift from its post-World War II pacifist stance by permitting exports of lethal military equipment. The move replaces earlier restrictions that allowed only non-lethal exports and aims to strengthen Japan’s defence industry and support projects like the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP). The policy includes safeguards restricting exports to 17 partner countries and requires compliance with UN norms.
keyPoints: Japan ended ‘five-category’ restriction on arms exports in April 2026
Now allows export of lethal weapons like missiles, destroyers, and fighter jets
Exports limited to 17 countries including India, Australia, Philippines, and the US
Decision linked to Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP) with UK and Italy
importantPoints: Article 9 of Japan’s Constitution renounces war and restricts military capabilities
Earlier exports limited to non-lethal categories: rescue, transport, warning, surveillance, minesweeping
GCAP aims to develop a 6th-generation stealth fighter by 2035
Japan’s National Security Council approval required for arms exports
India–Japan defence cooperation includes JIMEX, MALABAR, Dharma Guardian, and Veer Guardian exercises
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC GS under International Relations and Defence, especially Japan’s constitutional changes, global defence partnerships, and India–Japan military cooperation

items.title: World Earth Day 2026 Observed on April 22 with Theme ‘Our Power, Our Planet’
items.whyInNews: World Earth Day, also known as International Mother Earth Day, is observed on April 22, 2026, with the theme ‘Our Power, Our Planet’ focusing on renewable energy and community-driven environmental action.
items.summary: World Earth Day is a global environmental observance held annually on April 22 to promote awareness on climate change, pollution, and biodiversity loss. The 2026 theme emphasizes renewable energy transition and accountability for environmental damage. Originating in 1970, the movement led to key environmental laws and is now observed in over 190 countries.
keyPoints: World Earth Day observed annually on April 22 across 190+ countries
Theme for 2026: ‘Our Power, Our Planet’
Started in 1970 by US Senator Gaylord Nelson after Santa Barbara oil spill
UN General Assembly declared April 22 as International Mother Earth Day in 2009
importantPoints: Earth Day led to creation of US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)
Focus on climate change, pollution control, and biodiversity conservation
India’s Panchamrit commitments include net-zero emissions by 2070
India targets 50% renewable energy capacity by 2030
Key Indian initiatives: FAME scheme, Project Tiger, National Clean Air Programme (NCAP)
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC GS under Environment and Ecology, including international observances, climate commitments, and India’s environmental initiatives
